summary: The Docker build failed because a RUN step that downloads the nydus static tarball with wget returned a 502 Bad Gateway, causing the build step to exit with code 1 and buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: The RUN step executing wget to fetch the nydus release from GitHub failed with 'HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error', causing the command to exit with code 1 and the build to fail.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the wget command failed with a 502 response from the remote server.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Confirms the failing process was the wget command and that it exited with a non-zero status, causing the solve to fail.
Identifies the Dockerfile and line where the failing RUN wget command is located.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the top-level CI action (buildx bake) failed as a result of the wget/solve failure.

suggested_fix: Retry the workflow (this appears to be a transient HTTP 502). If failures persist, modify the Dockerfile to add retry/backoff to the download (e.g., curl/wget with --retry), use a cached or mirrored copy of the nydus artifact, or vendor the release into your build cache so the build does not depend on a single external host.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-11 F2 fix the shim didn't persist model_info into rows. This entry was backfilled from known run-time env: CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-mini, default base_url, default max_completion_tokens=4096. resolved_model and system_fingerprint are not recoverable from the original API responses.
